name: My back hurts from lifting boxes at work in Ogden, but it's not that bad—do I still need to report it?
description: Yes, report minor injuries right away—delays over 180 days in Utah disqualify you from workers' comp.[1]

mainEntity.name: My back hurts from lifting boxes at work in Ogden, but it's not that bad—do I still need to report it?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, report minor injuries right away—delays over 180 days in Utah disqualify you from workers' comp.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: **Yes, report even minor injuries immediately to avoid disqualification from benefits.** Utah mandates reporting within 180 days, and delaying can lead to claim denials even if symptoms worsen later.[1][2] Key factors: Employers must provide a safe environment, but injuries happen; documentation protects against disputes; physicians file Form 123 within 7 days. Tell your supervisor, get checked by a doctor, and keep records. Early reporting secures medical coverage and disability pay if needed.
